name: Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM)
training_approach: backpropagation through time (BPTT) with gated gradients
lineage_path: McCulloch-Pitts neuron -> Perceptron -> MLP -> RNN -> LSTM
summary: LSTM solved the vanishing gradient problem that crippled standard RNNs by introducing a gated cell architecture with forget, input, and output gates controlling information flow. Proposed by Hochreiter and Schmidhuber in 1997, it enabled learning of long-range dependencies in sequences and became the dominant architecture for speech recognition, machine translation, and text generation for nearly two decades. LSTM was the critical stepping stone that made sequence-to-sequence models and eventually the transformer possible.
